div.h1: Big-Endian vs Little-Endian: SHA-256 Word Storage

g.text: x86 stores LSB at lowest address
Block word parsing — all 16 words W[0..15]
Byte positions for W[0] = 0x6A09E667 (from abc block)
Writing H[i] to digest[] — big-endian byte order
Common bug: memcpy of native uint32_t
memcpy(digest, H, 32) on x86 | WRONG | reverses each word
store_be32 loop above | RIGHT | portable everywhere
htonl(H[i]) + memcpy | RIGHT | POSIX systems only
M1 padding length field | write_be64(buf+56, bits) | 64-bit length → 8 bytes MSB-first
M2 schedule W[0..15] | load_be32(block + t*4) | 4 bytes → uint32_t
M3 compression | uint32_t arithmetic only | no conversion needed mid-round
M4 digest output | store_be32(digest + i*4, H[i])| uint32_t → 4 bytes MSB-first

text.tspan: // x86 flipped the bytes for you
// x86 flipped the bytes for you
